chapter.title: The Path to the Holy Mountain

paragraphs.text: My dream was to imitate Saint John the Hut-dweller | Tur from the village of Aghios loannis | Thers's a village in Evia called Aghios Toannis; that’s where I'm from. My gerents were poor, and so my father left the village and went to America. He worked there as a labourer on the Panama Canal. In the village wwe children did chores from the earliest age. We would water the gar5 den and the trees; we would bring in the animals; we would do what: ever jobs the grown-ups told us to do. ; When Twas little T used to mind the flocks up on the mountain. [ was 14 slove-viitted and shy. I went to school for only one year, and we learnt vir: =zally nothing because the teacher was ill. While I was looking after the sheep, I read the life of Saint John the Hut-dweller, syllable by syllable. : Thats where my zeal to go and become a monk came from. Even though © mew nothing. | hadn't even seen a monk or a monastery. Nothing. . When I was seven my mother sent me to Chalkida to work in a shop. i sold everything. Shops at that time used to have ironmongery — keys, ‘ screws, locks and ropes and so on — but they'd also have sugar and rice | and coffee and pepper and everything you'd find in a grocers. It was a - ‘big shop. There were two other boys there. went and joined them. I did whatever they told me to. Everyone bossed me about and I was never off my feet running to and fro. Before I arrived, the two other boys had seorked out a system whereby they would go up to the balcony day and ¢ day zhout to water the basil plants belonging to the boss's wife. They had 2 sete When [arrived they got me to do everything — to sweep the floor and water the flowers. It didn’t cross my mind that it was unfair. I did ! ecerything. Wherever they told me to go, I went. : Ome day, just as I was finishing sweeping the shop, I noticed that | seme coffee beans had spilled on the floor next to where I had gathi zred my pile of rubbish. There would have been about fifteen beans in & ai I bent down and picked them up and walked over to the sack with : the coffee beans. My boss was in his office. It had windows all round : and he saw me bending down and then walking over to the sack. He : called out: | 1
‘Angelos!’ — meaning me, that's what they used to call me when I 4 “You should see the wine I drank on the Holy Mountain! I haven't was young — ‘come over here!” : found wine like that anywhere.
I went over. J “So you've been to the Holy Mountain, have you?” asked the other.
